sources.notes: Statement and constraints: choose one substring to shuffle so the whole even-length string becomes a palindrome; total length is at most 2e5.
Official editorial solution trims already matching mirror pairs, then binary-searches a prefix length of the remaining core in both orientations and checks character counts.

reference_candidates.notes: Luogu candidate source; not used in this review because the official editorial was sufficient.

annotation.summary: Find the minimum length of one contiguous substring whose characters can be arbitrarily reordered to turn an even-length string into a palindrome.
annotation.constraints: The string length sum is at most 2e5, so each feasibility check must be linear or near-linear and the number of checks logarithmic.
annotation.core_idea: First remove matching mirror pairs at both ends; they never need to be touched. In the remaining core, it is enough to try a shuffled prefix or, after reversing, a shuffled suffix. For a candidate length, consume from the shuffled multiset the characters required by fixed mirror positions; positions outside the shuffled zone must already match their mirrors, and the remaining multiset must have even counts.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) per total input batch with O(26) auxiliary count space per check.
tricks: Trim already matched mirror pairs before solving the core.
Check both orientations so a suffix shuffle is handled as a prefix shuffle after reversing.
Use multiset feasibility and leftover parity to test whether a shuffled zone can fill mirror demands.

solution_variants.name: trim-and-binary-search-window
solution_variants.summary: Trim equal mirrored characters, then binary search how long a prefix of the remaining core must be shuffled. The feasibility check matches fixed positions against the available character counts and requires even leftovers; reverse the core to cover suffix windows.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n log n) time, O(26) extra memory.

tags.evidence: The feasibility condition is entirely determined by mirror pairs in the target palindrome.
The minimum shuffled length is found by binary-searching a monotone feasibility predicate.
The check uses character counts of the flexible substring to satisfy mirror demands and validates leftover parity.
tags.description: Use frequency counts of a flexible multiset to test whether required matches, pairings, or parity constraints can be satisfied.
tags.created_reason: Existing frequency-pattern tags describe recognizing frequency shapes, while this problem needs the reusable multiset-feasibility check for reorderable characters.

tags.evidence: The solution trims equal mirrored pairs by advancing both ends before solving the remaining core.
